originalBehavior: The route opens as a long-form interactive comic with a splash scene, chapter dividers, anchor links, and scroll-driven reveals.
localStatus: The comic shell, chapter anchors, and splash flow remain intact, with public provenance removed from the visible header and credits surface.
notes: Panel markup and scroll-triggered iframe loading still come from the original runtime.
The header now keeps only the route title and timing details, while the shared footer carries the original-page reference.
evidence: Local walkthrough on 2026-03-10 confirmed the splash and chapter progression still run under the route root.
The vendored index continues to embed the original panel markup and iframe placeholders.

originalBehavior: The comic uses embedded simulations to show forgetting curves, spacing intervals, and why timed review beats cramming.
localStatus: The Ebbinghaus and calendar simulations remain upstream-driven and still load through the original iframe structure.
notes: The local port did not rewrite the core spacing or decay math.
These sections remain central to the article's teach-by-doing pacing.
evidence: Parity targets in the recheck plan explicitly called out forgetting-curve and active-recall timing scenes.
Source files still isolate the Ebbinghaus and calendar demos into their original simulation bundles.

originalBehavior: Readers flip cards, type answers, and interact with flashcard examples to practice active recall instead of passive review.
localStatus: Single-card, multi-card, and typed-card practice flows remain in the original simulation modules without host-page rewrites.
notes: The local cleanup focused on public attribution blocks, not the practice widgets themselves.
Share and footer changes do not alter the embedded flashcard controls.
evidence: The route still exposes the same practice-oriented card sections and typed prompts after vendoring.
Imported simulation directories match the published runtime layout.

originalBehavior: A guided Leitner-box section demonstrates review buckets, promotion or demotion rules, and spaced scheduling around a physical-card workflow.
localStatus: The Leitner-box interactive remains intact and still runs from the original HTML, CSS, and JS bundle.
notes: The tutorial still uses the upstream bucket visuals and card-movement logic.
No local cleanup was applied inside the Leitner runtime itself.
evidence: The recheck plan named the Leitner workflow as a parity-critical target for this route.
Local source retains the original dedicated Leitner simulation files.

originalBehavior: The comic closes with printable cards, download helpers, and extra practice resources that support taking the system offline.
localStatus: Printable PDFs and the download helper remain locally available, while the surrounding credits area now omits supporter and source-promo blocks.
notes: The local route keeps practical study resources reachable without preserving the upstream supporter panel.
Share controls remain available at the end of the article and now resolve from the local route root.
evidence: The vendored tree still contains PDF assets and the bundled download helper scripts.
The ending sequence in the local page retains the same resource-oriented structure after cleanup.
